title: Prefetch, Goroutine Concurrency, and Capacity · Go Build Room
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a measured consumer-capacity budget, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a measured consumer-capacity budget while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Prefetch Window Semantics
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 5490 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine prefetch, active goroutines, dependency capacity, and message-byte budgets; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Prefetch Window Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain rabbitmq per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global qos, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Prefetch Window Semantics, keep Prefetch Window Semantics connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

Prefetch controls the total number of ready messages stored in the queue. In Go, unbounded goroutines compensate for an oversized prefetch safely. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Prefetch Window Semantics happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
